summary: Every legal input has at most six decimal digits, which is too narrow to produce an honest runtime scaling verdict.

workload_bound.source_constraint: n is an integer between 1 and 100000 inclusive

replacement_checks.evidence: Each loop iteration removes exactly one decimal digit, performs constant-time accumulator updates, and the loop stops after d iterations.
Regression exhaustively checks all 100000 legal integers against an independent string-based digit oracle, including the six-digit upper boundary.
